steps.title: Find the median position
steps.content: With 33 fish total, the median is located at the middle position:$$\frac{33+1}{2} = 17$$So the 17th fish when sorted by weight determines the median.

steps.title: Determine which tank contains position 17
steps.content:
When all fish are ordered by weight:
  • Tank A (10 fish): positions 1–10, weights < 5 oz
  • Tank B (11 fish): positions 11–21, weights 5–13 oz
  • Tank C (12 fish): positions 22–33, weights > 13 oz
Position 17 falls in Tank B's range (11–21), so the median must be a weight from Tank B.

steps.title: Check which choice is in Tank B's weight range
steps.content:
Tank B contains fish weighing 5 to 13 ounces. Check each choice:
  • A) 4.5 oz: less than 5 ✗
  • B) 8 oz: between 5 and 13 ✓
  • C) 13.5 oz: greater than 13 ✗
  • D) 15 oz: greater than 13 ✗
That matches choice B — B is correct.
